text: Here are some best practices for designing REST APIs. First, use clear, noun-based endpoints like `/users` or `/orders` instead of verbs. Always return appropriate HTTP status codes, such as 200 for success, 201 for created resources, 404 for not found, and 500 for server errors. Good documentation is essential; tools like Swagger can help make your API easy to understand and test. Finally, implement versioning from the start. You can do this by including a version number in the URL, for example `/v1/users`. This allows you to make changes and add new features without breaking existing integrations. Following these practices makes your API more reliable and developer-friendly.
